content.full_text:
STATE OF CALIFORNIA
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
1102 Q Street • Suite 3000 • Sacramento, CA 95811
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

June 7, 2018

Ashley Titus
Bell, McAndrews & Hiltachk, LLP
Attorneys and Counselors at Law
455 Capital Mall, Suite 600
Sacramento, CA 95814

Re:    Your Request for Advice
       Our File No. A-18-122

Dear Ms. Titus:

     This letter responds to your request for advice regarding the conflict of interest provisions of the Political Reform Act ("Act")¹ on behalf of Town of Truckee Councilmember David Tirman. Please note that we are only providing advice under the conflict of interest provisions of the Act and not under other general conflict of interest prohibitions such as common law conflict of interest or Section 1090.

     Also, note that we are not a finder of fact when rendering advice (In re Oglesby (1975) 1 FPPC Ops. 71); any advice we provide assumes your facts are complete and accurate. If this is not the case or if the facts underlying these decisions should change, you should contact us for additional advice.

QUESTIONS

1) Does the public generally exception permit Councilmember Tirman to take part in discussions regarding the Town's participation in the Statewide Community Infrastructure Program ("SCIP") despite the fact the Councilmember Tirman's employer, JMA Ventures, has requested that the Town participate in the program so that it may take advantage of SCIP financing for a pending project?

2) May Councilmember Tirman take part in preliminary discussions involving financing administration program goals and policies?

CONCLUSIONS

1) No. The effect of the decision on Councilmember Tirman's interest in JMA Ventures is unique as compared to the significant segment of the public generally.

___________
¹ The Political Reform Act is contained in Government Code Sections 81000 through 91014. All statutory references are to the Government Code, unless otherwise indicated. The regulations of the Fair Political Practices Commission are contained in Sections 18110 through 18997 of Title 2 of the California Code of Regulations. All regulatory references are to Title 2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations, unless otherwise indicated.

File No. A-18-122
Page No. 2

2) No. Preliminary discussions involving financing administration program goals and
policies may determine, affirm, nullify, or alter decisions regarding the Town's participation in the
SCIP program. Accordingly, the decisions are "inextricably interrelated" and Councilmember
Tirman may not take part in the discussions.

FACTS AS PROVIDED BY REQUESTOR

You have asked whether the public generally exception permits Councilmember Tirman to
take part in discussions regarding the Town's participation in the Statewide Community
Infrastructure Program ("SCIP") despite the fact the Councilmember Tirman's employer, JMA
Ventures, has requested that the Town not participate in the program so that it may take advantage of
SCIP financing for a pending project. Additionally, you have asked whether Councilmember
Tirman may take part in preliminary discussions involving financing administration program goals
and policies. The initial facts pertinent to your question are more fully described in our previous
response, the Morris Advice Letter, No. A-18-078.

Supplementing the initial facts; you have stated that the discussion at hand for the Council is
not SCIP-specific, but rather a discussion to establish the goals and policies of public infrastructure
financing applicable Town-wide, including those goals and policies that must be discussed by the
Council prior to the Town undertaking a specific financing administration program. The Town will
be considering local goals and policies related to Community Facilities Districts and tax assessment
districts established under the Municipal Improvement Act of 1913 and the Improvement Bond Act
of 1914. The goals and policies established by the Town will provide a framework for Town
Council decision-making when project applicants make subsequent requests for Town sponsorship
of public infrastructure financing. Once goals and policies are established, specific projects will
come before the Council to be considered on their own merits on a case-by-case basis.

Additionally, you state that the decision to participate in the SCIP financing program could
apply to development on all commercial property or all real property, based on the final decision
made by the Town.

ANALYSIS

Public Generally Exception

The Act prohibits an official from taking part in a decision only if the decision's effect on
the official's interest is distinguishable from the effect on the public generally. (Section 87103.)
Commonly referred to as the "public generally exception," Regulation 18703(a) sets forth the
general rule:

"A governmental decision's financial effect on a public official's financial
interest is indistinguishable from its effect on the public generally if the official
establishes that a significant segment of the public is affected and the effect on his or
her financial interest is not unique compared to the effect on the significant
segment."

File No. A-18-122
Page No. 3

A "significant segment of the public" consists of at least 25 percent of: (1) the businesses or
nonprofit entities; (2) the real property, commercial real property, or residential real property; or (3)
the individuals within the official's jurisdiction. (Regulation 18703(b).)

While you state that the decision to participate in the SCIP financing program could apply to
development on all commercial property or all real property, the public generally exception does
not apply if there is a unique effect on the official's interest. Under Regulation 18703(c)(1), a
unique effect on an official's interest includes a disproportionate effect on the income producing
potential of the official's interest in a business entity. In this case, JMA Ventures is one of three
developers that have specifically requested that the Town participate in SCIP in the hopes of
utilizing the program to finance a pending project. Under these facts, JMA Ventures is uniquely
affected by any decisions regarding the Town's participation in the SCIP program and the public
generally exception does not apply.

Segmentation

Regarding participation in preliminary discussions involving financing administration
program goals and policies, an official can participate in preliminary discussions only to the extent
that the decision is not "inextricably interrelated" to a decision in which the official is disqualified.
(Regulation 18706.) Decisions are "inextricably interrelated' when the result of one decision will
effectively determine, affirm, nullify, or alter the result of another decision." (Regulation 18706(b).)
In this case, you have stated that the preliminary discussions contemplated "will provide a
framework for Town Council decision-making when project applicants make subsequent requests
for Town sponsorship of public infrastructure financing." Moreover, these preliminary discussions
were triggered by the request of three developers who are seeking to participate in the SCIP
program. Under these facts, it appears that the preliminary discussions are "inextricably
interrelated" to decisions regarding the Town's participation in the SCIP program. Accordingly,
Councilmember Tirman may not take part in these decisions.

If you have other questions on this matter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Brian G. Lau
Acting General Counsel

BGL:jgl
